kept.text:
$LUNR / $RDW / $FLY

NASA to increase the CLPS contracts maximum ordering value from $2.6 billion to $4.2 billion.

In a special notice published on April 27th, NASA/JSC announced it intends to issue a sole source contract modification to continue services for all activities necessary to safely integrate, accommodate, transport, and operate NASA Payloads using contractor-provided assets, including launch vehicles, lunar lander spacecraft, lunar surface systems, Earth re-entry vehicles and associated resources.

tweet.text:
How Space Data Centers Work?

Orbital AI superclusters running on unlimited solar power and natural vacuum cooling.

1. Orbital Deployment & Launch
Rockets and satellites delivering compute nodes into low-Earth orbit.
Tickers: $RKLB · $RDW · $LUNR · $PL

2. Power Systems (Solar + Energy Management)
Massive solar arrays harvesting constant sunlight with advanced power distribution.
Tickers: $NVDA · $GOOGL · $PL · $RKLB

3. Thermal & Cooling Management
Passive radiation cooling in the vacuum of space — no fans or chillers needed.
Tickers: $NVDA · $AMD · $HPE · $RDW

4. Compute Hardware & AI Integration
Radiation-hardened GPUs, TPUs, and onboard AI processing clusters.
Tickers: $NVDA · $AMD · $GOOGL · $HPE

5. Data Connectivity & Networking
Laser inter-satellite links and high-speed downlink to ground stations.
Tickers: $ASTS · $VSAT · $IRDM · $RKLB

Space data centers are floating AI superclusters — solving Earth’s power, cooling, and land limits by moving compute into orbit.
That’s why leaders in launch, hardware, and space infrastructure $NVDA, $RKLB, $ASTS, $GOOGL, SpaceX are positioned as the next wave of AI winners.

author.name: Jorge A.
author.description: Finance | Personalized 1:1 Coaching / En Español También | Book Your Session: https://t.co/tNvWGeT28O

quoted_tweet.text:
How Data Centers Stay Up? (2026 AI Edition)

What’s it like to build a fully functional data centre in the AI age?

1. Power Distribution
Gear that stabilizes, routes, and manages electricity (switchgear, PDUs, transformers, etc.).
Tickers: $ETN · $VRT · $SBGSY · $ABB

2. Backup Power
Instant failover when the grid fails—critical for 99.999% uptime.
Tickers: $GNRC · $CAT · $ETN · $VRT

3. Cooling & HVAC
The biggest bottleneck for AI racks. Air + advanced liquid/direct-to-chip systems to stop servers from melting.
Tickers: $VRT · $CARR · $TT · $EMR

4. Server Cabinets & Compute Hardware
Racks, enclosures, storage, networking—where the GPUs live.
Tickers: $HPE · $DELL · $SMCI · $VRT

5. Building Automation
Panels, sensors, energy monitoring, KNX/BMS for facility-wide control.
Tickers: $SBGSY · $LEGR · $CSCO · $VRT

6. Security Systems
Fire protection, cameras, access control, firewalls, and cyber hardware.
Tickers: $FTNT · $PANW · $CSCO · $SIEGY

A data center is basically a mini-city: power plants + cooling plants + automation + security + dense compute… all in perfect sync, optimized for AI workloads.

That’s why infrastructure leaders here (especially $VRT, $ETN, and cooling/power specialists) are among the biggest AI beneficiaries right now—riding hyperscale buildouts and liquid cooling transitions.

tweet.text:
$RKLB and $LUNR both had crucial back tests and bounces on Monday. 

$RKLB came down to the EMA20, bounced perfectly, and closed above the key structural level around $78.50. 

$LUNR also came down and bounced perfectly off the EMA20, before closing above the key structural level of $24.95. 

Healthy back tests within an uptrend for these two could bode well for the sector, as these are the names expected to lead. We are looking for follow through on Tuesday and if we see that, for other names like $RDW and $ASTS to catch a bid too.

tweet.text:
Very nice hammer on $LUNR yesterday. My thoughts wave 4 support reaction at $24.64–$25.02 looks great I bought calls and have my full position still. But, can't confirm a wave 5 until we reclaim $27.13–$27.70 with 4H/daily momentum RSI > 50.

On higher TFs, $LUNR still looks like an incomplete bullish structure imo, but lower TFs are still in repair mode, not confirmed w5 yet. Does the pullback / reclaim sequence confirm that $24.64–$25.02 was w4 support? Here's my two ideas and what I'm looking for:

Buy the Dip $LUNR — Scenario A

-Pullback / retest into the $24.64–25.02 shelf this is where 1D/4H hidden bullish div showed up.
-If price holds above the $24.20–24.26 daily BB mid / 20SMA zone that would be great!
-1H RSI holds 40–45 and curls, 4H RSI reclaims >50, and 4H MACD histogram improves.
-First reclaim trigger is $26.29–$27.13 then $27.51–27.70.
-Deeper dip version: price flushes toward $23.50 or even retests $22.57, 15m RSI goes oversold but recovers, 1H RSI curls from low 40s / upper 30s, and price quickly reclaims the $24.64–$25.02 shelf.
****Avoid BTD if price loses $25.02–$24.64 and cannot reclaim, 1H RSI breaks and stays below 40, 4H RSI remains below 50, and MACD histogram keeps expanding negative. Below $22.57, hidden bull div and my count gets invalidated.

Buy the Breakout $LUNR — Scenario B

-Break above $27.13, then hold / accept above $27.51–27.70 with volume.
-15m RSI should expand through 60 without immediate rollover, while 1H RSI >50, and starts with a 55–60 push (impulsive).
-Best confirmation is continuation through $28.21–$28.66 which opens door for a retest of $31.15.
-For w5 confirmation, I want 4H RSI >50 and 1D MACD histogram to print 2 positive bars while 1D RSI stays > 50.
***Fake break warning: price pokes above $27.13–$27.70, but 15m RSI is too stretched / rolls over, 1H or 4H RSI makes a lower high while price makes a higher high (bearish div), or volume fades into the breakout.
meaning we are still in w4 chop.

GL to you guys! $LUNR
